front: Work: autobiography titled after a Dunbar poem, mostly set in Stamps, Arkansas; the author went mute after being raped by Mr. Freeman at age eight
back: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings (Maya Angelou)
work: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings

front: Poem: read at Bill Clinton's 1993 inauguration; opens 'A Rock, a River, a Tree' and urges the listener to say 'good morning'
back: On the Pulse of Morning (Maya Angelou)

front: Poem: 'You may write me down in history / With your bitter, twisted lies'; asks 'Does my haughtiness offend you?'
Author: autobiography All God's Children Need Traveling Shoes recounts time as university administrator in Ghana/Accra
